titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Hub Braden Papers PASC.0152
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by UCLA Library Performing Arts Special Collections staff; machine-readable finding aid created by C. Clayton, Julie Graham, and Caroline Cubé.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated March 2026.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-17 15:34:16 -0700.

did.abstract: Hub Braden was an art director and production designer. The collection consists of script and production material, sketches, notes, and photographs related to his career.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Hub Braden Papers (Collection PASC 152). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by UCLA Library Performing Arts Special Collections staff.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Hub Braden was an art director and production designer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of script and production material, story boards (photocopies and originals), sketches, notes, and photographs related to the career of art director and production designer, Hub Braden. Includes materials from various television projects including All That Glitters, Baby I'm Back, Love American Style, Once Is Not Enough, and Irwin Allen's production of Alice in Wonderland. Also includes a small collection of what appears to be television wardrobe sketches (1960s or 1970s) collected by Braden.

did.unittitle: Days 1-12. Ext riverbank-ext Garden of Hearts. (Scenes 1-291c)
Days 13-20. Ext croquet field-Int banquet hall alcove. (Scenes 292-825c)
Day 21-30. Int banquet hall - Ext woods with caterpillar. (Scenes 838- 213)
Day 27-37. Ext woods - Ext woods and tree-owl. (Scenes 696-824a)
Day 38. Int. White Rabbit house - Ext. White Rabbit house. (Scenes 150- 193)
Day 39. Ext. edge of forest - Ext. clearing of castle wall. (Scenes 659- 789)
Day 40-41. Ext. Alice's garden - Ext. woods and clearing (Scenes 627-308)
Day 42-43. (Ext. woods and Mock Turtle - Ext. Checkerboard land and train. (Scenes 309-658)
Day 44-45. Ext. woods - Ext. path in woods and cave. (Scenes 679-695)
Day 46-48. Ext. path in woods - Ext. forest. (Scenes 241-374)
Day 49-50. Ext. forest with Lion and Unicorn - Ext. woods and path. (Scenes 734- 733)
Day 51-52. Ext. Forest with Red and White Knight - Ext. countryside. (Scenes 746- 764)
Additional scenes of Anteater and Frog. (Scenes 783-789). Includes blue prints.
Photographic effects sequences. Irwin Allen's production.
Production notes, correspondence, set lists, cast, staff and crew lists.
Script. (Part one) Written by Paul Zindel. Third draft, February 1985.
Script (part 2 - Through the Looking Glass). Written by Paul Zindel. Third draft, February 1985. Annotated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folders with details of what appears to be Jabberwocky head; sets for part I and II; interior and exterior of White Rabbit's house, and exterior for March Hare's Garden/Mad Tea Party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folders with pencil drawings of details of Jabberwocky head, interiors of Alice's house and banquet hall, and interior and exterior of White Rabbits house.

did.unittitle: Storyboards. Complete run-through (sc. 1-839). Irwin Allen's production.
#5. Written by Richard M. Powell and Harry Cauley. 02/24/77.
#16. Written by Kathryn Montgomery and Mark Morrison. 03/15/77.
#21. Written by Kathryn Montgomery and Richard M. Powell. 03/15/77.
#24. Written by Kathryn Montgomery, Michael Walker and Melodie Hollander. 04/01/77.
#35. Written by Martin Rips and Joseph Staretski. 04/02/77.
#36. Written by Roberta Edgar and Mary Thompson. 04/15/77.
#38. Written by Kathryn Montgomery and Harry Cauley. 04/15/77.
#39. Written by Martin Rips and Joseph Staretski. 04/15/77.
#41. Written by Martin Rips and Joseph Staretski. 04/27/77.
#42. Written by Martin Rips and Richard Staretski. 04/25/77.
#44. Written by Mary Thompson, Roberta Edgar and Harry Cauley. 04/28/77.
#46. Written by Martin Rips and Joseph Staretski. 05/03/77.
#54. Written by Martin Rips and Joseph Staretski. 05/11/77.
#59. Written by Martin Rips and Joseph Staretski. 05/20/77.
#61. Written by Martin Rips and Joseph Staretski. 05/20/77.
Gospel According to Angie, The. #56. Written by Sandy Krinski and Chet Dowling. 02/10/78.
Day at the Races. #58. Written by April Kelly and George Geiger. 03/03/78.
Odd Couple. #60. Written by Gary Jacobs. 03/24/78.
Olivia's Job Offer. #62. Written by Harry Cauley, April Kelly and George Geiger. 03/17/78.
You Bet Your Wife. #63. Written by April Kelly and George Geiger. 04/07/78.
Beat by a Drum. #51. Written By Martin Rips and Joseph Staretski. 12/19/77.
Living Proof. #52. Written by April Kelly and George Geiger. 12/22/77.
Farwell to Boyish Charm. #54. Written by Richard M. Powell. 01/20/78.
Loneliest Night of the Week. #55. Shelly Sellman and Wally Dalton. 01/27/78.
Photographs for 1979 pilot. Includes Alice Cooper, Frank Sinatra, Sammy Davis Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folder with pencil or what may be ink drawings on board of catapulte, ship at dock, and Cleopatra's fleet. (3 items)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folder with pencil or painted drawings on boards of schemes for Battle of Actium and exterior drawings of unidentified location. (3 items)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folder with pencil drawings mounted on boards of interior palace corridor of Alexandria, exterior of Alexandria Rome and caesars villa in Rome. (3 items)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folder with painted drawings on board of Cleopatra's garden home interior, exterior of procession in Rome, and flagship. (3 items)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folder with pencil and painted drawings on board of ships at sea, exterior of unidentified location, and ariel-type view of exterior of unidentified location. (3 items)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folder with pencil and painted drawings on board of exterior battle scene at wall, an unidentified exterior location, and interior of what appears to be Cleopatra's private quarters. (3 items)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folder with copied set plans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folder with pencil or what appears to be ink story board drawings of Cleopatra's fleet. (4 items)

did.unittitle: Set and model photography. Includes Horton house and hospital.
Starring Carol Burnett. Revised script 10/19/81. Annotated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folders with pencil drawings of exterior set scene that include dwellings and shops.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Oversize map folder with pencil and copied drawings.

did.unittitle: Unfinished Business. By Margaret G. Oberman. Table draft, 04/25/85.
Long Way Down, The. By John McNamara. Table draft, 04/26/85
Love and the Class Reunion. By Richard Albrecht and Casey Keller. Table draft, 04/25/85. (2 copies)
Plain Truth, The. By Arthur Sellers. First draft, 04/09/85.
Much Too Personal. No author, no date.
Clarke's restaurant photographs. Exteriors. Includes other building facades.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Appears to represent variety television programs ca. 1960s or 1970s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Appears to represent variety television programs ca. 1960s or 1970s. May contain a small number of items relating to the Sonny and Cher Show.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Appears to represent variety television programs ca. 1960s or 1970s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Appears to represent variety television programs ca. 1960s or 1970s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Appears to represent variety television programs ca. 1960s or 1970s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Original storyboard sketches for unidentified project. 219 leaves.
